info.name: QuantiNova API – Full Stack (Gateway + AI Core + MCP + OpenClaw)
info.description:
Complete API smoke-test collection for the Dynamic Yield Optimization Agent stack.

Covers:
  • Gateway REST /api (portfolio, auth, optimize, execute, agent chat)
  • Gateway Web3-native /v2 (chains, universe, positions, oracle, pool, swap, bundles, MEV relay, opportunities, activity, autonomy)
  • AI Core / TRANSACT HTTP (VaR, moments, explain, funding, factors, scenarios, asset search)
  • Yield-Agent MCP Server (all 7 JSON-RPC 2.0 tools)
  • OpenClaw agent chat proxy

Variables to set before running:
  gatewayUrl  – default http://localhost:3000
  aiCoreUrl   – default http://localhost:8000
  mcpUrl      – default http://localhost:3001
  wallet      – any valid EVM address
  optimizationId – set after running 'Optimize (start)'

item.name: 0 – Health Checks
item.description: Verify all services are up before running other requests.

request.description: Returns {status:'ok', service:'gateway-wdk'}
Returns {status:'ok', service:'yield-agent-mcp'}
Redis-backed health check. Returns {status:'ok'} if Redis is connected.

item.name: 1 – Gateway Legacy /api
item.description: Original REST API used by the React frontend and proxied by the Yield-Agent MCP server.

request.description: Generate a nonce for Sign-In with Wallet (EIP-4361). Pass walletAddress, use returned nonce in auth/verify.
Verify EIP-4361 wallet signature. Returns a session token on success. Replace signature and message with real values from your wallet.
WDK read-only portfolio. Returns native + ERC-20 balances (USDT, USDC by default) with USD values. Results cached 120 s in Redis.

Triggers async optimization via gRPC to ai-core. Returns optimizationId immediately. Use 'Execute – Fetch plan' after ~3-5s to retrieve the result. Subscribe to ws://localhost:3000/ws/progress?optimizationId=<id> for live progress.

Retrieve the cached optimization plan from Redis. Returns 404 if the plan is not yet ready (ai-core still computing) or has expired. Run 'Optimize – Start optimization' first and set {{optimizationId}}.

Broadcast an already-signed raw transaction hex. Non-custodial: server never signs. Replace signedTxHex with a real signed tx from your wallet. Gateway calls eth_sendRawTransaction on the specified chain.

Gateway proxies this to OpenClaw at OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL (default http://openclaw:18789). Returns 503 if OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL is not set or OpenClaw is down. Requires chat completions enabled in OpenClaw.
Gateway proxies /api/transact/* to AI_CORE_HTTP_URL (ai-core:8000). Same as hitting aiCoreUrl/funding directly.
Gateway proxies /api/transact/* to AI_CORE_HTTP_URL.

Open this URL in Postman's WebSocket tab (or wscat) after starting an optimization. Streams JSON progress events: {status, progress, summary, plan?}. States: FETCHING_DATA → COMPUTING_GRAPH → GENERATING_PLAN → WAITING_FOR_SIGNATURE → DONE.

item.name: 2 – Gateway Web3-Native /v2
item.description: Web3-native REST + WebSocket surface for chain discovery, oracle pricing, pool state, swap simulation, MEV relay, bundles, activity, and agent autonomy.

request.description: Returns runtime chain registry. Used by the frontend and agent UI to populate chain selectors dynamically. Each entry includes id, name, kind (evm/solana/etc), chainId, configured.

Builds a single structured snapshot for UI and agent consumption. Query params: chains (comma-separated slugs), assets (symbols/names/addresses), quote (default USDT), include (tokens,prices,positions). Add wallet param when include=positions.
Same as universe snapshot but also fetches wallet positions. Requires wallet query param.
v2-shaped portfolio response backed by gateway portfolio service (WDK read-only). Alias for /api/portfolio with v2 response envelope.

Returns off-chain oracle price for a token pair (e.g. ETH-USDT). Priority: Pyth Hermes (fresh ≤30s) → CoinGecko fallback. If both available, reconciles and rejects if deviation >2%. Resolves both legs dynamically (no hardcoded pair mappings).
Same as oracle price but with optional dex param for context.
Fetch pool state via EVM eth_call. Tries Uniswap V2 getReserves() first (reserve0, reserve1), then Uniswap V3 slot0() (sqrtPriceX96, tick). Default poolAddress is USDC/WETH Uniswap V2 pool.
Real Uniswap V2 USDC/WETH pool on Ethereum mainnet.
Uniswap V2 swap simulation via eth_call to router getAmountsOut. Returns expectedOut (wei), slippage (vs oracle reference), mevRiskScore. Router address from UNISWAP_V2_ROUTER_<CHAIN> env var.
Bundle simulation stub. Returns simulatedPnl and adversarialOrderings preview. Stub implementation — full adversarial reordering is a future enhancement.
Submit a Flashbots bundle via eth_sendBundle. Ethereum mainnet only. Uses FLASHBOTS_RELAY_URL (default https://relay.flashbots.net). Replace signedTxHex with real signed transactions.

item.name: MEV Protect – Submit via Flashbots Protect

request.description: Route signed tx via MEV-protected relay. protection: 'flashbots-protect' uses FLASHBOTS_RPC_URL, 'mev-blocker' uses MEV_BLOCKER_RPC_URL, omit for public RPC. Appends txHash to Redis activity log for walletOrSessionId.

item.name: MEV Protect – Submit via MEV Blocker

request.description: Returns DeFi opportunities from the Neo4j knowledge graph via ai-core /assets/search?type=opportunity. Results include protocol, asset, APY, risk score, liquidity.
Redis-backed transaction audit trail. Each item includes txHash, chain, timestamp, explorerUrl. Populated by /api/execute/signed and /v2/protect/submit (when walletOrSessionId is provided).
Persist agent autonomy state in Redis for a given wallet/session. Used by the frontend UI toggle and future enforcement hooks. TTL-based eviction.
Open in Postman WebSocket tab. Sends connected message + periodic heartbeats. Full streaming can be wired to live indexer updates.
Open in Postman WebSocket tab. Sends initial opportunities payload and refreshes periodically.
Open in Postman WebSocket tab. Sends agent_status payload including autonomous state if set in Redis.

item.name: 3 – AI Core / TRANSACT (port 8000)
item.description: Unified Python container: gRPC on 50051 + TRANSACT FastAPI on 8000. Accessible directly or via gateway proxy at /api/transact/*. TRANSACT_API_URL is mandatory — ai-core will not start without it.

item.name: VaR – Value at Risk + Expected Shortfall

exec: pm.expect(j).to.satisfy(r => r.var_historical !== undefined || r.var_parametric !== undefined || r.VaR !== undefined);

request.description:
Compute VaR and Expected Shortfall from a T×N returns matrix. Parameters:
  returns: T×N matrix (T time periods, N assets)
  weights: portfolio weights summing to 1 (default: equal weight)
  alpha: tail probability (default 0.05 = 95% VaR)
  portfolio_value: NAV in USD for dollar VaR
  horizon_days: holding period
  include_monte_carlo: also compute MC-based VaR
  mc_simulations: number of MC paths

item.name: Moments – Portfolio return, vol, skew, kurtosis

exec: r.portfolio_return !== undefined || r.mean !== undefined || r.expected_return !== undefined

request.description: Compute portfolio statistical moments: annualised expected return, volatility, skewness, excess kurtosis. Essential for characterising DeFi return distributions (fat-tailed, positively skewed). Use as input for HRP/MVO strategy selection.
Returns a natural-language explanation of a quant formula from the Neo4j knowledge graph (seeded via pdf_ingest from AlgorithmicTradingStrategies/ PDFs). Powers the MCP explain_formula tool.
Fetch perpetual/spot funding rate data for a symbol. include_series=true returns the full historical series for delta-neutral strategy analysis.
Returns on-chain risk factors for a chain/protocol: TVL momentum, utilization rate, liquidation risk. Used as factor inputs for MVO and Black-Litterman views.
Search for yield opportunities from the Neo4j knowledge graph. This endpoint is proxied by /v2/opportunities in the gateway.

Simulate the P&L impact of adversarial MEV bundle ordering on a portfolio position. slippage_bps: expected slippage from sandwich/frontrunning in basis points. asset_indices: indices in the returns matrix that are exposed to MEV.

Simulate the effect of an oracle price spike on portfolio volatility and liquidation risk. vol_mult: multiplier applied to the target asset's volatility (e.g. 2.0 = 2x vol spike). asset_index: which column in returns to spike.

Multi-chain Monte Carlo simulation. use_t_dist=true uses Student-t distribution (fat tails, better for DeFi). df: degrees of freedom for t-distribution. n_paths: number of simulation paths. Returns path percentiles and terminal wealth distribution.

item.name: 4 – Yield-Agent MCP Server (JSON-RPC 2.0)
item.description: MCP server at port 3001 exposes 7 tools for OpenClaw agent orchestration. Transport: HTTP JSON-RPC 2.0 (POST /mcp or POST /). TRANSACT_API_URL must be set or the server will refuse to start.

request.description: MCP protocol initialize handshake. Returns protocolVersion, serverInfo, and capabilities.
List all registered MCP tools. Expects 7 tools: get_portfolio, run_optimization, get_optimization_plan, broadcast_signed_tx, quant_var, quant_moments, explain_formula.
Fetch DeFi portfolio (native + ERC-20 balances) for a wallet. Calls GET /api/portfolio on the gateway. Results are cached 120 s in Redis. Non-custodial: read-only via WDK.

Start a yield optimization run. Calls POST /api/optimize on the gateway, which triggers gRPC to ai-core. ai-core queries the Neo4j knowledge graph and calls TRANSACT for VaR/moments enrichment. Returns optimizationId immediately — use get_optimization_plan to poll the result after ~3-5 s.

Fetch the cached optimization plan from Redis. Run 'run_optimization' first and ensure {{optimizationId}} is set. If the plan is not ready yet, wait 3-5 s and retry. Returns actions array with protocol, asset, chain, amount, expectedApy, riskScore and a summary with TRANSACT-enriched metrics.

Broadcast an already-signed raw transaction hex. Non-custodial: the server never signs. User signs the tx in MetaMask/WDK and passes signedTxHex here. Gateway calls eth_sendRawTransaction on the specified chain. Replace signedTxHex with a real signed tx for live testing.

item.name: MCP Tool – quant_var (VaR + ES via TRANSACT)

request.description: Compute portfolio VaR and Expected Shortfall via TRANSACT /risk/var. Required for quant-level DeFi risk assessment. returns: T×N matrix, weights: portfolio allocation, alpha: tail probability (0.05 = 95% confidence), portfolioValue: NAV in USD.

item.name: MCP Tool – quant_moments (return, vol, skew, kurtosis via TRANSACT)

request.description: Compute portfolio statistical moments via TRANSACT /portfolio/moments: annualised return, volatility, skewness (positive = right tail), excess kurtosis (positive = fat tails). High kurtosis is typical for DeFi — prefer HRP over MVO in this case.
Get a natural-language explanation of a quant formula from the Neo4j knowledge graph (seeded from AlgorithmicTradingStrategies/ PDFs). Calls TRANSACT /agents/explain.

item.name: MCP – Batch request (portfolio + var in one call)

request.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 batch request. MCP server processes all requests concurrently and returns an array of responses.

item.name: 5 – End-to-End Flows
item.description: Ordered sequences that simulate a full user/agent interaction. Run requests within each subfolder in order.

item.name: Flow A: Portfolio → Optimize → Plan (REST)
item.description: 1. Get portfolio | 2. Start optimization (auto-sets optimizationId) | 3. Wait ~4s | 4. Fetch plan

item.name: Flow B: MCP Agent Flow (OpenClaw simulation)
item.description: Simulate an OpenClaw agent conversation via direct MCP calls. 1. tools/list | 2. get_portfolio | 3. run_optimization | 4. get_optimization_plan | 5. quant_var | 6. explain_formula
